specification.summary: Implement the Article Hero block with JS, CSS, and tests

outputStructure.metadataRow: div.article-hero-metadata containing: category badge (linked), date (<time>), separator, read-time
outputStructure.imageSection: div.article-hero-image containing responsive picture (only if image present)

variants.noImage: article-hero no-image — no image section, title collapses upward

decorateFunction.postInit: Add 'article-hero-loaded' class to block after decoration complete

specification.missingMetadata: If getMetadata() returns null/empty for any field, omit that element entirely from the DOM. No exceptions thrown. Other fields render independently.

responsive.mobile: < 600px: metadata items stack vertically (flex-direction: column, align-items: flex-start), separator hidden
responsive.desktop: >= 600px: metadata items inline (flex-direction: row), separator visible

acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: Category badge renders with correct text, linked to category page
AC-2: Published date displays in 'MONTH DD, YYYY' format with <time> element
AC-3: Read time displays 'X MINUTE READ'
AC-5: With-image variant: responsive hero image renders with eager loading
AC-6: No-image variant: no image section present, layout adjusted
AC-9: Missing metadata: element omitted, no exceptions, other fields unaffected
AC-10: Responsive (< 600px): metadata row items stack vertically; separator hidden
AC-11: CSS uses design system tokens
AC-12: ESLint 0 errors, Stylelint 0 errors, block tests pass
constraints: EDS:DECORATE-PATTERN — decorate(block) reads metadata via getMetadata() from aem.js
EDS:RESPONSIVE-IMAGES — Hero image must use createOptimizedPicture(), never manual
EDS:DESIGN-TOKENS — All visual properties from CSS custom properties
EDS:ACCESSIBILITY — Semantic h1, <time> element, linked category with meaningful text
EDS:VANILLA-JS — No frameworks, no build step, vanilla JS/CSS only
GOV:STOP — Search before creating (completed: no existing article-hero found)
